content.full_text:
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
428 J Street • Suite 620 • Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886


May 27, 2004


Gregory W. Sanders
6585 Buckley Drive
Cambria, CA 93428

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. W-04-099

Dear Mr. Sanders:

This letter is in response to your request for advice on the conflict-of-interest provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").¹ The Act requires the Commission to provide written advice to any person whose duties under the Act are in question or to that person's authorized representative. (Section 83114(b); regulation 18329(a), (copy enclosed).) However, the Commission may decline to provide written advice if it would be inappropriate or otherwise not in the public interest to do so. (Regulation 18329(b)(8)(F).)

Formal written advice is the application of law to a given set of facts provided by the requestor and has the purpose of conferring immunity to the requestor. (Section 83114; regulation 18329(b)(7).) After a careful review of the facts you provided, we decline to provide written advice to you at this time. The same or very similar issues are currently the subject of a pending investigation by the Enforcement Division. In addition, your current question involves analysis of a series of interrelated decisions, some of which have already occurred, such that issuing advice at this time would relate to past conduct and, therefore, would not further the impartial, effective administration of the Act so mandated by section 83111.

___________________________

1. Government Code sections 81000 – 91014. Commission regulations appear at title 2, sections 18109 – 18996, of the California Code of Regulations.

File No. W-04-099
Page No. 2

Please note that our decision is not a determination of the question posed in your request.

Should you have any questions, please contact the Commission at (916) 322-5660.

Sincerely,

Luisa Mendiola
General Counsel

C. Scott Tocher
Staff Counsel, Legal Division

Enclosure
CST:jg

sections.question_synthetic: The requestor asked for advice on conflict-of-interest provisions under the Political Reform Act regarding a series of interrelated decisions, some of which had already occurred.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC declined to provide written advice because the same issues are the subject of a pending Enforcement Division investigation and the analysis would relate to past conduct rather than future guidance, which would not serve the impartial administration of the Act.
sections.parsing_notes: Format: ocr; LLM: Declination letter with clear conclusion section. Question section not explicitly labeled but can be inferred from conclusion. Document references pending investigation as basis for declination. OCR quality is generally good with minor artifacts.

CONCLUSION: After a careful review of the facts you provided, we decline to provide written advice to you at this time. The same or very similar issues are currently the subject of a pending investigation by the Enforcement Division. In addition, your current question involves analysis of a series of interrelated decisions, some of which have already occurred, such that issuing advice at this time would relate to past conduct and, therefore, would not further the impartial, effective administration of the Act so mandated by section 83111.

embedding.summary: The FPPC declined a request for advice on Political Reform Act conflict-of-interest provisions, citing a pending investigation by the Enforcement Division and the fact that the analysis would relate to past conduct rather than future guidance.
